programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When two hikers are discovered murdered near a remote coastal trail, the team is on the hunt for a fugitive duo wanted in a series of grisly campsite murders.

programme.desc: When Malo joins the NYPD, he is taken in by his murdered father’s old partner and a team of rogue cops.

programme.title: Law Abiding Citizen
programme.desc: A man decides to take justice into his own hands after a plea bargain sets one of his family's killers free.

programme.title: Den of Thieves
programme.desc: A cop and an ex-military gang leader clash when the Outlaws plan a heist at L.A.'s Federal Reserve Bank.

programme.title: Agent Red [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Captain Matt Hendricks and Dr. Linda Christian are locked in a submarine with Russian terrorists that threaten to launch a chemical virus on US territory.

programme.title: Thrill Seekers
programme.desc: When intrepid reporter Tom Merrick discovers that the same man appears in photographs of major disasters over a hundred-year period, he embarks on an incredible action-filled race against time to prevent a series of new disasters.

programme.title: Mercenary for Justice
programme.desc: As a mercenary, John Seeger (Steven Seagal) has a reputation that precedes him. That's why he's being blackmailed into performing a risky job: breaking the son of a wealthy arms dealer out of a South African prison. With his friends taken hostage, Seeger agrees to do the deed -- but finds out too late that he's been double-crossed. Not one who likes being taken for a fool, Seeger hatches an elaborate plan to get even that will make the prison break look like capture the flag.

programme.title: Bordertown [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Jennifer Lopez is a tenacious reporter investigating the murders of young women working in a bordertown.

programme.title: Code Name Banshee
programme.desc: Caleb, a former government assassin in hiding, resurfaces when his protégé, the equally deadly killer known as Banshee, discovers a bounty has been placed on Caleb's head. Now, they must put the past behind them and join together one last time to fight off the secret CIA-backed killers who threaten to destroy them.

programme.title: The Quick and the Dead
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a homesteading family's life when they are attacked by a ruthless gang.

programme.title: Flyboys
programme.desc: World War I aerial battles

programme.title: Georgia Rule
programme.desc: A troubled young woman is sent to live with her grandmother for the summer, while hiding a secret that could potentially tear her family apart.

programme.title: Cold Comes The Night
programme.desc: CHLOE (Alice Eve), a struggling motel owner and her daughter SOPHIA are taken hostage by TOPO (Bryan Cranston), a nearly blind career criminal as he attempts to retrieve his cash package from corrupt cop, BILLY (Logan Marshall-Green). Hoping to escape to a better life, Chloe tries to strike a deal with Topo but soon finds herself in over her head, and a series of double crosses leaves a cloud of mayhem and murder.

programme.title: Southland Tales
programme.desc: Set in Los Angeles in the year 2008 during a three day heat wave just before a huge 4th of July celebration, an action star stricken with amnesia meets up with an adult film star developing her own reality television project and a Hermosa Beach police officer who holds the key to a vast conspiracy.

programme.title: Money Monster
programme.desc: Lee Gates, a bombastic television stock huckster, is taken hostage on his TV show by an outraged gunman. As much of the world watches live, Gates must keep himself alive and, with help from the woman who produces the show, uncover the truth behind a tangle of big money lies

programme.title: Chappie
programme.desc: Chappie tells the story of a robot imbued with artificial intelligence who is stolen by two local gangsters who want to use him for their own nefarious purposes.

programme.title: Cactus Flower
programme.desc: A bachelor talks his assistant into posing as his wife so he can avoid marrying his girlfriend.

programme.title: Twentieth Century
programme.desc: Carole Lombard and John Barrymore star in this all-time classic screwball comedy based on the Charles MacArthur-Ben Hecht Broadway hit and directed by Howard Hawks. It s the story of a maniacal Broadway director (Barrymore) who transforms shopgirl Carole Lombard from a talented amateur to a smashing Great White Way success adored by public and press.

programme.title: To Sir, With Love
programme.desc: An unemployed engineer takes a job as a teacher of hostile and rebellious students in their last year at a high school in London's tough East End.

programme.title: Theodora Goes Wild
programme.desc: Riotous comedy and zany romance fuel this story of a small-town girl (Irene Dunne) who writes a racy best-selling novel and falls in love with the New York sophisticate (Melvyn Douglas) who illustrates it. A fine supporting cast keeps this film rolling along splendidly.

programme.title: Two For The Money
programme.desc: After suffering a career-ending knee injury, a former college football star aligns himself with one of the most renowned touts in the sports-gambling business.

programme.title: The Killer [Dubbed]
programme.desc: A disillusioned assassin accepts one last hit in hopes of using his earnings to restore vision to a singer he accidentally blinded.

programme.title: Jobs
programme.desc: College drop-out Steve Jobs teams with childhood friend Steve “Woz” Wozniak to assemble the first Apple Computer in his parents’ garage. Driven by a relentless pursuit of excellence, Jobs transforms the world of computing at the cost of his personal life.

programme.title: Fong Sai Yuk 2 [Subbed]
programme.desc: The Cantonese hero Fong Sai Yuk becomes involved in the secret brotherhood "The Red Flower", who are trying to overthrow the Manchurian emperor and re-establishing the Ming dynasty. The social upheaval is combined with Sai Yuk's personal moral conflict about how to conform to the rigid regime of the brotherhood and on top of that sort out his difficult love life, saddled with two presumptive wives

programme.title: The Legend of Hercules
programme.desc: Banished by the king, Hercules harnesses his powers and gathers an army to fight his way back to his kingdom.

programme.title: Robin Hood

programme.title: Power Rangers

programme.title: Hellboy
programme.desc: Caught between the worlds of the supernatural and human, Hellboy battles an ancient sorceress bent on revenge.

programme.title: Taken From Rio Bravo
programme.desc: Two friends pursue a group of human traffickers that have kidnapped five women in 1874, East Texas.

programme.title: Andromeda: 1883
programme.desc: Space marine Casey Reynolds travels into the past to stop a killer android bent on wiping out the one person crucial to saving the human race.

programme.title: Pinkerton
programme.desc: Allan Pinkerton, founder of America's first detective agency, embarks on a mission to capture the notorious outlaw, Jesse James.

programme.title: Best Man Dead Man
programme.desc: A bachelor party in the middle of nowhere quickly turns into a night of murder and mystery when the best man ends up with a knife in his head. Everyone is a suspect; only an eccentric private eye in town can solve the mystery.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 3
programme.desc: Two female survivors join forces to bring down the deadly group responsible for unleashing a hungry shark in a West Texas lake.

programme.title: Rise of the Legend
programme.desc: A powerful martial arts master (Eddie Peng) returns to the town where his father was murdered to take on a ruthless crime boss (Sammo Hung).

programme.title: Kung Fu League
programme.desc: When Fei Ying's manager stops him from getting the girl he loves, he magically summons help from kung fu legends.

programme.title: The Foggy Mountain
programme.desc: A fearless martial-arts master seeks revenge for the murder of his wife.

programme.title: Spiritwalker
programme.desc: Unable to remember anything about his life, a man begins regaining consciousness in a new body every 12 hours. Now, he must piece together his identity, all while evading attacks from pursuing agents and dangerous criminals alike.

programme.title: Hansan: Rising Dragon
programme.desc: In 1592, Korea faces the threat of the powerful Japanese navy. As defending Adm. Yin Sun-sin's forces fall into crisis, they resort to a secret weapon: the dragon-headed ships known as geobukseon. Using them, they hope to turn the tide of the war.

programme.title: The Plot to Kill My Mother
programme.desc: Young woman in witness protection seeks mother's killer, questioning her reality.

programme.title: You Killed My Mother
programme.desc: Teenage Jo Fay attacks nurse over mother's transplant denial, later leaves asylum.

programme.title: Cellmate Secrets
programme.desc: Mitchell's former manager and prison security supervisor are speaking out.

programme.title: My Mother Stole My Life
programme.desc: A mother steals her estranged daughter's identity to escape an abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: The Plot to Kill My Mother
programme.desc: Young woman in witness protection seeks mother's killer, questioning her reality.

programme.title: The Love Issue
programme.desc: A beauty editor finds a fake boyfriend when her ex-boyfriend (and boss) gets engaged.

programme.title: Hearts Under the Olive Tree
programme.desc: Suffering from writer’s block, an author heads to a farmland retreat to get inspired.

programme.title: Eat, Love, London
programme.desc: Stella takes a job at a new London restaurant and ends up falling for the chef.

programme.title: A Slice of Romance
programme.desc: PR exec Mikayla strikes a deal with pastry chef Austin to rebrand his café to get dating advice.

programme.title: A Match for the Prince
programme.desc: A matchmaker risks falling for a real-life prince before his royal wedding date.

programme.title: The New Girl
programme.desc: When her sister Monica suddenly disappears under suspicious circumstances, Malorie becomes convinced that Monica's seemingly perfect husband is hiding something sinister. She then launches her own investigation and unearths a twisted web of lies, abuse, and even political conspiracy. Malorie must confront dangerous secrets that not only threaten her sister's life but her own.

programme.title: CODE
programme.desc: Dallas artist MALIK SCHAEFER, also known as CODE, is a rising star in the underground rap scene. Although his checkered past has led him down the wrong road at times, with the help of his best friend, STYLES, Malik is back on the fast-track to success. That is, until he reconnects with the dangerous love of his life, SOPHIA ORTEGA, who threatens to destroy it all. Malik must decide to either follow his heart or live by the code that has served him well for many years: family over everything.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: Call Her King
programme.desc: After sentencing a notorious gangster to death, Judge Jaeda King must rely on her wits, strength and skill when her courtroom is taken over by force by the prisoner's brother.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Revisiting the Heart
programme.desc: Azia Steed is a lead homicide detective who handles a heavy caseload while juggling her personal life. After taking some time off, she runs into an old fling, Dr. Ezrah Jacobs III. Azia receives a case involving the murder of a known local basketball star. The more evidence is uncovered, the stronger the web of deception becomes.

programme.title: One More Day
programme.desc: Raquel Ferguson, is an up and coming photographer and single mother to a 15 year old daughter. As her business grows, it causes more strain on the relationship between them. Raquel is driven by the need to be better than her mother, who she has never forgiven for things in the past. Raquel is unable to see the hypocrisy in her own life, as her relationship with her daughter deteriorates right under her nose. When a terrible tragedy occurs, Raquel begs God for a miracle.

programme.title: Heart and Soul Food
programme.desc: Three sisters fight for their mom's approval, unaware that their mother is dealing with a struggle of her own.

programme.title: Pages of Love
programme.desc: Single mother, Samantha, finds herself in a cycle of never-ending bad dates. Will Samantha listen to her mom, the relationship expert, and take her advice on forgiving and moving forward?

programme.title: What Momma Says
programme.desc: Nearly thirty, Paul Thomas, is still living with his mom. When Paul cannot pay his half of the rent, she kicks him out, forcing Paul to sink or swim when it comes to taking care of himself.

programme.title: Ice Sharks
programme.desc: As scientists study the effects of climate change on sea life, a new breed of shark breaks through.

programme.title: Mega Piranha
programme.desc: A mutant strain of giant ferocious piranha escape from the Amazon and eat their way toward Florida.

programme.title: The Land That Time Forgot
programme.desc: A modern adaptation of the classic novel about survivors of a shipwreck trapped on an island with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Disaster Strike Force
programme.desc: The entire California coastline is threatened as a 20.0 earthquake looms.

programme.title: Age of Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Dinosaurs escape a biotech firm and terrorize Los Angeles. A father must rescue his daughter in the chaos.

programme.title: Itsy Bitsy
programme.desc: A story tangled up in a web of family drama, personal demons, and a horrifying monster.Based on the centuries-old poem, a family moves into a secluded mansion where they soon find themselves being targeted by an ancient entity taking the form of a giant spider.Kara, a private nurse and single mother, moves from the big city to the quiet countryside with her two children, 13-year-old Jesse and 8-year-old Cambria, to live with and take care of an elderly man with multiple sclerosis. Things seem normal enough until Jesse discovers a mysterious old relic in the old man's house, leading to unexpected and horrifying things coming to life.

programme.title: The Isle (Edited Version)
programme.desc: When three shipwrecked sailors land on an island abandoned except for four sole residents, one sailor starts to question what happened on the island. He must uncover the truth while he battles to save his own life and escape the isle.

programme.title: Bloodsucking Bastards (Edited Version)
programme.desc: When an office worker at a soul-crushing company loses a promotion to his longtime nemesis, he begins to fear for more than his job as strange disappearances and bizarre deaths suggest the office is turning into a haven for the undead.

programme.title: The Legend of the Psychotic Forest Ranger
programme.desc: After taking a wrong turn down an abandoned road, the Jock, the Joker, the Blond Bimbo & the Paranoid Brunette run out of gas and find themselves stranded in the middle of the woods. What a total drag. What was that noise? Why is everyone vanishing? Is it a practical joke or could the Legend be true? Your compass wont save you in these woods.

programme.title: Itsy Bitsy
programme.desc: A story tangled up in a web of family drama, personal demons, and a horrifying monster.Based on the centuries-old poem, a family moves into a secluded mansion where they soon find themselves being targeted by an ancient entity taking the form of a giant spider.Kara, a private nurse and single mother, moves from the big city to the quiet countryside with her two children, 13-year-old Jesse and 8-year-old Cambria, to live with and take care of an elderly man with multiple sclerosis. Things seem normal enough until Jesse discovers a mysterious old relic in the old man's house, leading to unexpected and horrifying things coming to life.

programme.title: A Dark Song
programme.desc: A heartbroken woman and recluse occultist perform an ancient, dark ritual to bring her child back to life - but missteps have consequences and unknown evils are unleashed.

programme.title: From Black
programme.desc: A recovering drug addict, saddled by crushing guilt after the disappearance of her young son, is presented with a bizarre offer to learn the truth about what happened and set things right.

programme.title: Mandrake
programme.desc: Mandrake follows probation officer Cathy Madden, who is given the task of rehabilitating notorious killer 'Bloody' Mary Laidlaw back into society after twenty years of jail.

programme.title: The Noonday Witch
programme.desc: After movingto a rural village, Eliska must protect her daughter from the mysterious woman who haunts the surrounding farm fields.

programme.title: Prevenge
programme.desc: Prevenge

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this heartfelt Mother’s Day episode of Chewed Up, Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly reflect on the women who shaped them while Carla leads the kitchen with a show-stopping mimosa brioche French toast topped with frangipane. Inspired by her mother’s love of simple breakfast classics like pancakes and French toast, Carla elevates the dish into a baked, brunch-worthy centerpiece featuring brioche, apricot jam, almond-rich frangipane, and a champagne-orange glaze. As the dish comes together, the hosts share nostalgic stories about their childhoods—from weeknight dinners and Sunday sauces to special outings at places like Bob Evans and Chinese restaurants—highlighting how food connects to family memories. The conversation flows with humor and warmth, touching on generational differences in cooking habits and dining culture. Once the dish is baked to golden perfection, the trio digs in, praising its rich texture and balance of flavors, with Carla’s creative twist winning everyone over. The episode continues with playful segments like “What Would Mom Say,” where the hosts share funny and revealing lessons from their mothers, followed by fan mail and a casual food discussion. The show wraps with a giveaway of Carla’s personal artwork and a sincere tribute to moms everywhere, celebrating the love, lessons, and meals that define family traditions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in the kitchen as he prepares a nostalgic Quiche Lorraine inspired by his mother, making the case for a simple yet meaningful Mother’s Day brunch made at home. As he walks through the process—layering raw onions, crispy bacon, and a rich egg-and-cream custard into a par-baked crust—Carla Hall and Michael Symon chime in with their own takes on brunch traditions, ingredient preferences, and the beauty of regional cuisine. The trio shares laughs over everything from vintage baby names to the infamous “Real Men Don’t Eat Quiche” era, using humor to dismantle outdated ideas around food and masculinity. Along the way, they highlight the importance of texture and balance, celebrating the silky, slightly jiggly custard as the hallmark of a perfectly baked quiche. A fan interaction segment adds a personal touch, with a “Fan of the Day” selected for a custom video message, reinforcing the show’s community-driven spirit. Once the quiche comes out of the oven, the hosts dig in, offering real-time reactions and thoughtful observations about flavor, technique, and versatility—debating whether quiche is best suited for brunch, lunch, or dinner. The episode wraps with a heartfelt nod to mothers everywhere and a reminder that sometimes the simplest dishes, made with care, are the most memorable.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon takes a nostalgic trip back to his early culinary days, recreating an OG state fair classic—steak on a stick—while Carla Hall and Clinton Kelly dive into a lively, humor-filled conversation about food, memories, and the joy of eating with your hands. Using flank steak, Michael walks through his simple but flavor-packed marinade of soy sauce, Worcestershire, Dijon, and spices, explaining the importance of cutting against the grain and letting the meat soak overnight for maximum tenderness. As the skewers hit the grill, the hosts debate whether food is inherently more fun on a stick, with Clinton firmly in the “yes” camp and Michael offering a more chef-driven perspective. The conversation branches into strong opinions on kebabs, with the trio agreeing that mixing vegetables and meat on the same skewer is a mistake, leading to soggy results. Along the way, they share personal anecdotes—from Carla being mistaken for Tracee Ellis Ross to Michael’s frustrations with diners’ rigid expectations around classic dishes—highlighting the humor and unpredictability of food culture. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting the finished steak, praising its bold flavor and simplicity, while also announcing a fan giveaway tied to Michael’s Simply Symon show. As always, the group signs off with their signature blend of warmth, wit, and encouragement to keep cooking and having fun in the kitchen.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon reunite in the kitchen for the very first episode of CHEWED UP. Carla makes Sweet Potato Hummus, Michael debates the name, Clinton avoids cooking, and the chaos kicks off immediately.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon dive into a tailgating-themed episode of CHEWED UP with Michael leading the charge. He fries up crispy Pork Schnitzel Sliders while sharing wild football stories, Carla admits she’s barely tailgated in her life, and Clinton once again finds himself assigned kitchen tasks he may or may not be qualified for. Between talk of The Chew’s abrupt cancellation, psychic predictions, and potato roll “squish” debates, the chaos continues.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton ushers in sweater weather with a decadent, over-the-top hot chocolate while the crew revisits the infamous "Eggnog-gate" story - a stomach-turning behind-the-scenes tale from their network TV days. Cozy vibes meet barely appropriate storytelling.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off the week with Crispy Baked Chicken Wings tossed in a hot honey and lime glaze. The hosts look back on the freeing final weeks of The Chew before its abrupt end, and Michael delivers a passionate rant against air fryers. The trio introduces their new interactive "Fan Fridge" segment, and Clinton shares a hilarious story about how eating messy wings on a date made him realize he was gay!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall brings the heat with a comforting Nashville Hot Chicken Mac and Cheese. Carla builds a heavily spiced, vibrant cheese sauce and folds in crispy chicken and large elbow pasta. The hosts reminisce about Carla's famous ground nut stew from Top Chef, discuss their competitive streaks, and debate the merits of retro candies like Peeps and the 100 Grand bar.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly embraces fall with a recipe for Pumpkin Spice Muffins that double as cupcakes when topped with frosting. While the treats bake, the hosts debate whether muffins belong at breakfast or dessert and share their absolute worst live television disasters including Michael cooking during an active fire and Clinton slicing his finger on live TV.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall kicks off Monday with an appetizer: Deviled Eggs Three Ways, including a beautiful beet-soaked variation. Things go hilariously off the rails when Carla accidentally adds too much liquid to her yolk filling, turning it into a soupy mess. The hosts embrace the chaos and try to fix it, while also chatting about the Wicked movie, hating cottage cheese, and giving away a trio of Pop Mustard.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly elevates lunchtime with savory French Onion Soup Tartines, topped with melted Gruyere. While the onions caramelize, the trio discusses their love for French food, debates the perfect early dinner time for getting older, and shares hilarious stories about awkward fan encounters including the time a woman on a crowded train told Clinton he looked "so much fatter in person".

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes the lead in the kitchen as she introduces a playful and unexpected dish: fried Kool-Aid, inspired by nostalgic state fair treats from her childhood. As she walks through the process of making the vibrant red dough using Kool-Aid powder, flour, sugar, and a leavening agent, Clinton Kelly reacts with skepticism while Michael Symon shares his own less-than-great experience trying the dish at a fair. The conversation quickly turns humorous as Carla recounts a bizarre childhood memory of having a runny nose at the fair, prompting playful teasing from the group. As the dough is fried into donut hole-like bites, the hosts discuss technique, including how the dough naturally flips in hot oil and how to tell when it’s perfectly cooked. Carla elevates the dish by tossing the warm bites in lemon sugar and offering a Kool-Aid glaze for dipping, balancing sweetness with a bright citrus note. Once tasted, both Clinton and Michael are pleasantly surprised, praising the light texture and more refined flavor compared to traditional fair versions. The episode transitions into a lively fan mail segment where the hosts debate their least favorite vegetables and share personal preferences, followed by a special “fan of the day” moment celebrating a crew member’s mother’s birthday with jokes and a spontaneous song from Clinton. The episode wraps with the hosts encouraging viewers to engage with the show and teasing future fun.

programme.title: Behind the Counter
programme.desc: Behind the Counter: Dave Chang makes Silence of the Lambs and Scream inspired dishes, and behind the scenes at Majordomo - In this episode, we give you a tour of Majordomo Studios and a behind the scenes look at Chef David Chang's creative process as he and his team come up with ideas for the Halloween episode of Dinner Time Live, a weekly live cooking show on Netflix. Dave riffs on concepts from Silence of the Lambs and Scream. The Majordomo team celebrates Halloween. Andrew makes family meal.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - A Little Bit of Everything: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on "A Little Bit of Everything" menu for comedian Jay Pharoah and rapper Lil Jon. Dishes include: Tempura Rock Shimp with Salsa Seca, Everything Bagel-Spice Flatbread, and Boiled Chicken Two Ways.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Ratatouille Tarte Tatin" - Chris Ying creates a beautiful ratatouille tarte Tatin, motivated by a combination of online influences, including an on-the-fly challenge from his buddy David Chang while they were making chicken pot pie together in a different video. Chris riffs on the popular trend zipping around social media to make tarte Tatin (typically a sweet pastry) while paying homage to recipe YouTuber extraordinaire, Andrew Rea (Binging with Babish), who's first mega-popular video was recreating the ratatouille from...Ratatouille.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "McDonald's General Tso's Chicken" - Chris Ying has seen all of the McDonald's "hacks" that are floating around social media, and he's calling BS. Most of these don't even qualify as actual hacks, so he takes matters into his own hands and concocts a clever dish using Chicken McNuggets, Sweet & Sour sauce, ketchup, (and a few other things you'd have in your kitchen) to truly upgrade your McDonald's meal.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Mango Pie with California Mangoes and Graham Cracker Crust" - Chris Ying finds himself with four really expensive, fancy mangoes, so he tries out a viral mango-pie recipe from Samin Nosrat and Hrishikesh Hirway.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Silky Spicy Eggplant" - Chris is joined by beloved cookbook author Hetty McKinnon to test out “EASY & QUICK VEGAN EGGLANT RECIPE IN CHINA” from TikTok/IG sensation @foodiechina888 (a.k.a. Wayne Shen).

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Chris Makes Beef & Broccoli" - Who doesn't love broccoli beef (other than vegetarians)??? Lucky for you, Chris Ying woke up with a craving for THAT EXACT DISH. Here he shows you how to make his dead simple version (using the microwave to speed things up). This is some high-quality weeknight cooking, people! Make it for yourself and let us know what you think.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Hetty McKinnon's Eggplant Mapo" - Hetty McKinnon, an Australian Chinese cookbook author and James Beard Award finalist, stops by the studio to show Chris Ying how to make her Eggplant Mapo—a delicious twist on Mapo Tofu.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Nico de Leon's Inasal Fried Chicken Sandwich" - Chef Nico de Leon shares his recipe for a delicious twice fried Inasal Chicken Sandwich, bringing in Filipino flavors and plenty of pro tips for creating a beautiful sandwich.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Invitation to a Banquet: A Conversation with Fuchsia Dunlop" - Step into the vibrant world of Chinese cuisine with acclaimed author and culinary adventurer, Fuchsia Dunlop, as she unveils the flavors and stories behind her latest book, "Invitation to a Banquet." Dunlop stops by the Majordomo Media studio to chat with Chris Ying to discuss her lastest literary achivement and some perspectives from her 30+ years of studying Sichuan food and Chinese cuisine.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Fresh Bok Choy Kimchi with Monica Lee" - The wonderful Monica Lee of Beverly Soon Tofu is back in the kitchen with her daughters to share her recipe for fresh Bok Choy Kimchi. This is an easy way to have a big batch of homemade kimchi in the fridge, or split it in half to use some to make a quick banchan. This recipe and many more are available in Monica's new book, Sohn-mat: Recipes and Flavors of Korean Home Cooking.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Chris Ying Makes His Signature "Pickled" Green Beans with Ground Pork" - The most popular recipe in the history of the Recipe Club podcast, Chris Ying's "Pickled" Green Beans with Ground Pork are a perfect accompaniment to rice. Salty, sour, packed with umami, and a little bit spicy, they beat the pants off Aunt Clarissa's green-bean casserole. For the spice-heads, Chris adds a hit of chopped, pickled jalapeño at the last minute for a little kick.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "David Chang Shares 3 Quick and Easy Recipes: Meatballs, Braised Chicken and 'Hugo' Pasta" - Dave cooks up three of his easiest, go-to recipes that are all endlessly riffable, can be prepped ahead and frozen, and make good use of some clever microwave techniques. Pork meatballs over pasta, braised chicken, and "Hugo" pastaâ€”a delicious twist on pesto pasta that kids love.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

Dishes include: Chicken Scallopini with Mushrooms and Marsala / Chicken Scallopini with Mortadella Cream Sauce / Chicken Scallopini with Radicchio and Gorgonzola Cream Sauce.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen
programme.desc: Nick remembers his glory days of playing football when he came to America, as he showcases elegant dishes to serve for your Sunday Football parties. programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen
programme.desc: Nick talks about how he had to grow as a chef while preparing soups and the realization of the warm comfort in finishing a soup recipe. Dishes Include: Mussel Soup with Brandy and Saffron / Lentil Soup with Sausages / Butternut Squash Soup with Cannellini Beans and Ham.

programme.title: Nick Stellino: Storyteller in the Kitchen

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. The team first uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George and three side-kicks head straight into the “Bearcage”, looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones that Texas and Oklahoma can throw at them. They find it – and their stormchasing vehicles don’t look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George's exploration of the undersea world begins on a submarine in Grand Caymon.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: The “Angry Planet” team will be tracking storms through the 2009 Hurricane season, and plan to film george as he heads into a big one.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: For George Kourounis‚ ground zero for stormchasing is Oklahoma City, the capital of´Tornado Alley´.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: The Angry Planet team heads to Africa to climb the steep and very active Nyiragongo Volcano in the Congo that erupted in 2002, sending 125,000 people fleeing for their lives. Host/adventurer George Kourounis explores the bubbling lake of molten lava and investigates the risk the dangerous volcano poses to both the people of city of Goma and the Mountain Gorillas who live on the nearby jungle slopes.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: The autumn of 2006 brings hurricanes to the North Atlantic and hot dry winds fan forest fires across Northern Ontario. Host/adventurer George Kourounis has traveled to some awesome hurricanes, including Rita and Katrina, with his pal Mark Robinson. This year they stormchase in Newfoundland, site of the hurricane that pulled Mark out of a life-threatening depression.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Host George Kourounis has braved many terrifying phenomena – but none as frightening as the one he takes on in this episode: marriage! To make sure his new bride is on the same adventurous page with him, he proposes to get married on the lip of an exploding volcano, on the South Pacific island of Tanna, in Vanuatu. Before the native preacher, dressed in grass skirt and kustom garb, gets to perform the smoky vows, George descends into the erupting volcano to bring out red hot samples of the lava it is producing.
